description: Hot work permit active in Gas Processing while CO readings rose from 32 to 44 ppm over 20 minutes. Permit was not suspended despite approaching threshold. Near-miss recorded when shift supervisor noticed the trend during handoff.
outcome: Permit suspended manually, area ventilated. No injury.
lessons: Hot work permits should auto-suspend when gas readings exceed 75% of threshold in the same zone.

description: Confined space entry in Steam Plant boiler inspection. Gas detector alarmed at 38 ppm CO during entry. Crew evacuated. Source traced to a leaking valve that was not isolated per procedure.
outcome: Crew evacuated safely. Valve isolated. Entry permit suspended for 48 hours.
lessons: Confined space pre-entry checklist must include independent verification of isolation.

description: Blast Furnace A had 7 workers in zone during a tap-hole maintenance operation when CO levels spiked to 46 ppm. High worker density combined with gas elevation created compound risk.
outcome: Workers donned RPE. No casualties. Maintenance completed under enhanced monitoring.
lessons: Worker density limits should be dynamic based on current gas readings, not static headcount caps.

description: Identical pattern to NM-2024-001 but with additional maintenance crew. Three risk factors stacking simultaneously. This was the precursor pattern observed before the Vizag incident.
outcome: Near-miss flagged during safety audit. No action taken at the time.
lessons: CRITICAL: This pattern was documented but no automated intervention existed. Compound risk detection engine would have fired at score 60+.

description: Welding permit active in Coke Oven Bay. Adjacent gas line developed a slow leak (6 ppm/min increase). Welding continued for 12 minutes before gas alarm triggered.
outcome: Gas leak detected by area monitor. Welding stopped. Line isolated.
lessons: Welding permits near gas lines should trigger continuous cross-zone gas monitoring.
